title: Baghali Polo ba Mahicheh (Fava Bean Rice with Lamb Shank)
description: A magnificent Persian centerpiece featuring tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb shanks served alongside aromatic saffron rice studded with dill and fava beans.

instructions.text: Wash and soak the basmati rice in salted water for 1 hour.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a pot. Brown the lamb shanks on all sides, then remove.
In the same pot, sauté the onion and garlic until soft. Add turmeric, black pepper, and half the salt.
Return lamb shanks to the pot, cover halfway with water, and simmer gently until tender.
In a separate pot, boil water, add the drained rice, and boil for 5 minutes. Add fava beans in the last minute, then drain.
Layer the parboiled rice and beans with fresh dill in a pot. Pour the bloomed saffron over the top.
Cover with a towel-wrapped lid and steam on low heat to create tahdig. Serve alongside the shanks.

tips: Grind saffron with a tiny pinch of sugar and dissolve in hot water (bloom) for the best color and aroma.
storage_notes: Store lamb and rice in separate airtight containers in the fridge for up to 3 days.
serve_with: Salad Shirazi, Mast-o-Khiar, and Sabzi Khidan (fresh herbs).

substitutions.note: Use 1 cup dried dill if fresh is not available, but fresh yields better flavor.
You can use beef stew meat, cooking until tender.
